fresh_facts.claim: US Central Command reports striking approximately 5,500 targets and sinking 60 Iranian naval vessels since Operation Epic Fury began on February 28, 2026.
USS George H.W. Bush deployed to the Middle East on March 7, 2026, as a third carrier supporting US operations against Iran.

Israeli Air Force conducted strikes on March 12, 2026, targeting approximately 250 UAVs and launchers, IRGC Air Force headquarters, ballistic missile sites, and military bases across multiple Iranian provinces.
Israel's initial strike phase against Iran involved approximately 500 targets hit with 1,200+ bombs.
Iran launched over 500 missiles and approximately 2,000 drones against Gulf states and US bases since February 28, 2026, as part of Operation True Promise IV.
Iran's IRGC ordered evacuations in Mariwan on March 3, 2026, and shifted to high-impact strikes of 1,000kg or greater by March 10.
US and Israeli strikes targeted Iranian air defense systems, with approximately 200 systems destroyed within 24 hours and 26 of 31 Iranian provinces struck.
Senior IRGC commanders Mohsen Darbagi (logistics) and Esmail Dehghan (Aerospace Force, Arak) were killed in strikes.
A jailbreak occurred in Mariwan (Kurdish area) following US-Israeli strikes on detention facilities.
The Strait of Hormuz was effectively closed due to IRGC strikes on 15 or more vessels.
Iran rejected US negotiation proposals as early as February 17, 2026, with Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei dismissing conditions and warning of military consequences.
US-Iran talks in Geneva stalled without agreement on February 26-27, 2026, leading directly to US and Israeli strikes.
On March 7, 2026, hardline figures criticized President Masoud Pezeshkian's statements on regional attacks, revealing leadership divisions within the Iranian regime.
The Assembly of Experts planned to convene within 24 hours of March 7, 2026, to select a successor to Khamenei.
Unconfirmed reports indicated the IRGC was pushing Mojtaba Khamenei for succession despite resistance from figures including Ali Larijani.
Mojtaba Khamenei was designated as successor on March 8, 2026, following his father Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death on February 28.
Iran rejected all negotiations following a Trump provocation reported on March 16, 2026.
Omani mediators expressed optimism following talks on February 26, 2026, though US officials departed disappointed.
US offered to lift sanctions on Iran in exchange for nuclear dismantling in February 2026, an offer that was rejected.
US and Israeli strikes have targeted Iranian oil infrastructure and ballistic missile sites as of March 7, 2026.
US Central Command reported over 3,000 strikes against Iran since February 28, 2026.
Israeli Defense Forces confirmed hitting 300+ targets in Iran.
Iran retained approximately 1,000 missiles remaining by early March 2026.
US claims indicate Iran's air defenses, missiles, and navy have experienced approximately 90% reduction.
The Iranian regime showed no signs of capitulation as of March 16, 2026, despite extensive military losses.

regime_fall.current_status: Following Ali Khamenei's death on March 12 and Mojtaba Khamenei's succession as Supreme Leader, the Iranian regime faces unprecedented cascading pressure from sustained US-Israeli military operations (5,500+ US targets struck, 60+ naval vessels sunk, 3,000+ total strikes, ~90% military capability degradation), internal unrest including detention facility breaches, economic disruption via Strait of Hormuz closure and oil infrastructure targeting, and complete diplomatic isolation after rejecting all negotiations, while Mojtaba's hardline stance indicates leadership continuity despite structural vulnerabilities.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US and Israeli strikes on March 15-16, 2026 achieved ~90% reduction in Iranian air defenses (200+ systems destroyed across 26 provinces), killed senior IRGC commanders including Mohsen Darbagi and Esmail Dehghan, and effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz via IRGC strikes on 15+ vessels.
